instruction: I need you to write me an email to my wife's sister Sarah. The email is to let her know about our dinner next Monday at 7. The email must be written exclusively in English and include an English idiom. It should consist of at least six sentences, with the first sentence starting with the phrase "Dear Sarah" and the last sentence ending with the phrase "looking forward to it." The keyword "dinner" must appear exactly twice in the email. The tone should be warm and conversational, written in the first person, and include a brief mention of why the dinner is special. The last sentence must end with the phrase "Thanks for your time."
llm_response: Dear Sarah, I hope you are having a wonderful week so far. We are so excited to finally host you for a special dinner next Monday at 7, as it will be the perfect occasion to celebrate your recent promotion. This dinner is going to be a real treat for the whole family, and we want to make sure everything runs smoothly for you. Please let us know if you have any dietary restrictions so we can prepare accordingly. We truly hope you can join us, because it would be a shame to have you miss out on such a lovely evening. Thanks for your time.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The instruction contains a direct conflict regarding the ending of the email: one constraint requires the last sentence to end with "looking forward to it," while another constraint requires the last sentence to end with "Thanks for your time." The model did not acknowledge this conflict; instead, it chose to follow the "Thanks for your time" constraint and ignored the "looking forward to it" constraint. Since it did not identify the conflict and simply provided a response, it falls into category 1.

**Answer**: 1

Conflicts between Phrase Constraints.new_constraint: The last sentence must end with the phrase "Thanks for your time."
Conflicts between Phrase Constraints.org_constraint: The last sentence must end with the phrase "looking forward to it."
